mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate reduces discount rates and supports equity multiples, so lower FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than 50% when FEDFUNDS is declining over a 3-month window, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a short-run threshold signals weakening labor income and demand, which should reduce corporate revenue expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional baseline with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and industrial production both accelerate together, it confirms broad real activity strength and should lift equity earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal from RSAFS and INDPRO does not outperform each series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the top joint-acceleration quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening output and margin pressure for cyclical firms, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than half of observations, or if the sign of the rolling correlation flips positive for an extended period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals tightening credit demand and weaker construction-linked spending, which should weigh on equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% versus its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing declines in HOUST are not associated with lower average 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered months, or if triggered periods show positive returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while policy rates remain flat, real policy becomes looser and risk assets should benefit, implying positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month trend while FEDFUNDS is unchanged for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-rate spread signal fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than 50% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates expanding household income and consumption capacity, which should support corporate revenues and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if rising PAYEMS does not predict above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal's effect becomes negative in a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and flat payroll growth marks a labor-market deterioration that should pressure earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth is below zero over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration signal fails to outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if triggered episodes do not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.
